summary: Soundboard recording with clear mix captures a legendary Terrapin Station jam and high‑energy post‑song improvisations.
review: The tape delivers solid soundboard quality—generally clean with only a slight pitch‑sharpness on the Terrapin that most listeners overlook. Musically the show shines thanks to an epic Terrapin Station performance, extended Wheel/Dark Star excursions and a wildly praised Wave to the Wind jam, even if the first set and Easy Answers are viewed as weaker spots.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight exceptional Terrapin Station and post‑song jams; a few note pitch issues and a sub‑par first set, yielding an overall strong but not flawless impression.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced soundboard capture. Reviewers mention the mix is good, though one notes Terrapin Station runs slightly sharp in pitch. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as slow or uneven, but the second half erupts with a monster Terrapin Station jam that feeds into extended improvisations (Wheel, Dark Star, Wave). The post‑Terrapin segment is repeatedly called one of the best jams of the ’90s.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is highlighted as shredding and a key driver of the Terrapin jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is noted as part of a tight, locked‑in rhythm section during the improvisations.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboard) receives praise for fiery synth work on Easy Answers and the deep Terrapin jam; midi effects are called perfect for this era.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums/percussion are repeatedly lauded, especially in the post‑Terrapin space where they provide a chunky, rhythmic backbone.
